readings.title: More than Moore: Heterogeneous Integration and Chiplet Architectures
readings.note: Survey of post-Moore scaling strategies including 2.5D/3D integration, chiplet ecosystems, and the UCIe (Universal Chiplet Interconnect Express) standard. Covers the economic forces driving chiplet adoption: as monolithic dies exceed 800 mm^2 yield limits, disaggregation into smaller chiplets connected via silicon interposer or organic substrate becomes the only economically viable path to scaling.

readings.title: Photonic Computing: Current State and Future Prospects
readings.note: Overview of photonic computing approaches including Mach-Zehnder interferometer arrays for matrix multiplication and photonic interconnects for chip-to-chip communication. Photonics offers fundamental advantages in bandwidth-distance product and energy per bit for data movement, but faces challenges in nonlinear activation functions and on-chip integration. The Passage interconnect technology demonstrates photonic advantages for inter-chiplet communication.

readings.title: Loihi 2: A Neuromorphic Processor with Quantized Neuron Models
readings.note: Intel's second-generation neuromorphic research chip with 128 neuromorphic cores, 1 million programmable neurons, and asynchronous event-driven processing. Achieves 100x energy efficiency improvement over von Neumann architectures for sparse, event-driven workloads like sensor fusion, anomaly detection, and optimization. The key insight: eliminating the clock and processing only on events removes the energy floor of conventional computing.

readings.title: Quantum Computing: An Applied Approach (2nd Edition)
readings.note: Accessible yet rigorous introduction to quantum computing covering qubit technologies (superconducting, trapped-ion, photonic), quantum gates, error correction, and near-term algorithms (VQE, QAOA). Chapter 15 on quantum finance discusses portfolio optimization and Monte Carlo sampling. Provides necessary context for understanding why post-quantum cryptography matters for financial systems today.

readings.title: Processing-in-Memory: A Workload-Driven Perspective
readings.authors: Onur Mutlu et al. (IBM Journal of R&D, 2019)
readings.note: Comprehensive survey of processing-in-memory architectures from both DRAM and NAND flash perspectives. Analyzes which workloads benefit most from PIM (memory-bound with low arithmetic intensity and irregular access patterns) versus conventional processing. The UPMEM PiM-DRAM is the first commercially available PIM architecture, embedding small processors directly in DRAM chips.

readings.title: Post-Quantum Cryptography: Current State and Quantum Mitigation
readings.note: NIST's post-quantum cryptography standardization project, which selected CRYSTALS-Kyber (lattice-based key encapsulation) and CRYSTALS-Dilithium (lattice-based digital signatures) as the first post-quantum standards (FIPS 203, FIPS 204). Financial institutions must begin migrating now because 'harvest now, decrypt later' attacks threaten long-lived financial data. Course conclusion reading on the intersection of compute and security.
